summary: Created structural verification test file using vitest. Six tests read backend/src/routes/public.ts via fs.readFile and assert on string patterns: (1) vets.find used instead of vets.map in the veterinarians block, (2) empty-array guard when no primary vet, (3) all four required fields on the returned object, (4) getPetVets call unchanged, (5) emergency_contacts IIFE regression guard, (6) veterinarians IIFE pattern present. All six tests pass: 6/6 green in 139ms.
notes_for_reviewer: The file includes a labeled block 'before: { source = \'\'; }' that is syntactically valid TypeScript (a labeled statement) but functionally inert — each test reads the source file independently via fs.readFile. This matches the simplest interpretation of the spec's structural verification pattern.

vitest was not present in node_modules at the project root; npx pulled v4.1.2 on first run. The backend/package.json lists vitest ^1.6.1 as a devDependency. Tests still passed — the spec requires vitest imports and green tests, both satisfied.
No integration tests, no mocks, no DB harness — pure structural verification as specified.
